name: Komi-Permyak
notes: Komi-Permyak (перем коми / коми-пермяцкӧй кыв) of Perm Krai, written in a Cyrillic alphabet extended with ⟨ӧ⟩ and ⟨і⟩. The grapheme inventory is NOT yet specified. Komi orthography encodes consonant palatalisation through the following vowel letter and the soft sign, in a system that differs from Russian; a reliable letter → IPA table requires a descriptive source that has not been consulted. The historic Molodtsov alphabet is not covered. Metadata only. PHONEME INVENTORY: attested inventory from PHOIBLE (glottocode komi1269), declared directly. `graphemes` stays empty because none of that depends on the writing system: the sounds of this language are known regardless of whether — or how — it is written.

sources.author: Moran, Steven & McCloy, Daniel (eds.)
sources.title: PHOIBLE 2.0
sources.publisher: Max Planck Institute for the Science of Human History
sources.notes: Attested phoneme inventory for glottocode komi1269.

ancestors.notes: Uralic — the classification node this language sits under.
